content:
First we index the set 
  M={1,2,4,5,7,8,10,11,…}  
by letting a₁=1, a₂=2, a₃=4,… .  One checks easily the closed‐form  

 a_m  = m + ⌊(m–1)/2⌋.  

We look for 2n consecutive terms a_r,…,a_{r+2n–1} whose sum is 300.  A straightforward “run‐of‐the‐­mill’’ telescoping‐floor calculation shows that there are exactly five solutions, namely  

n=1 (then r=100),  
n=2 (r=49),  
n=4 (r=22),  
n=5 (r=16),  
n=10 (r=1).  

Hence the only positive n for which 2n consecutive elements of M can sum to 300 are  

 n ∈ {1,2,4,5,10}.  

Answer:  \boxed{1,2,4,5,10}.
